title: Code review fixes — date validation, dead code, theme loader duplication, findConfig boundary
description: Fix all issues identified in the post-MVP code review. One bug is user-visible (invalid dates written silently), the rest are dead code and structural cleanup.

features: FIX 1 — src/commands/add.js: validate --from and --until before writing. After parsing opts.from / opts.until, call validateDate() from config.js on each. If invalid, print 'Error: --from: not a valid ISO 8601 date — got "<value>"' and exit 1. Do not write to links.yaml.

FIX 2 — src/generator.js: remove the local loadThemeCSS() function and replace it with an import of loadTheme() from ./themes/loader.js. The two functions are identical — use the single source of truth.

FIX 3 — src/utils.js: remove the dead loadConfig() function and CONFIG_FILE constant (never imported anywhere, CONFIG_FILE uses wrong .yml extension). Keep only isLinkActive() and the fatal() helper. Update the file JSDoc header to reflect the reduced scope.
FIX 4 — src/config.js: remove the DEFAULT_CONFIG export (defined but never imported anywhere). If a default config object is needed in future it should be introduced alongside the code that uses it.

FIX 5 — src/config.js: update findConfig() to stop climbing at a git root boundary. After resolving the candidate path, also check if a .git directory or .git file exists in the current dir — if so and links.yaml is not found there, stop and throw instead of continuing upward. This prevents silently picking up a parent project's links.yaml.
outputs: src/config.js (cleaned — DEFAULT_CONFIG removed, findConfig git-boundary added)

notes: FIX 5 boundary logic: in the while loop, after failing to find links.yaml in `dir`, check if `path.join(dir, '.git')` exists (existsSync). If it does, throw — do not climb further. This is the standard git-root-stop pattern. Do not throw if .git exists AND links.yaml also exists in the same dir (that case is fine).
